message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Implementing multi-file support for click.edit() (edit_file -> edit_files). Need to own these two files.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Adding process_priority param to click.edit()/Editor class. I need to own _termui_impl.py and termui.py. What is your feature touching?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Our features both touch the same 2 files (Editor class + edit() signature), so a fully disjoint split isn't possible. Proposal: sequential ownership. I'll take both files now, implement multi-file edit support (small, localized change: edit_file->edit_files, filename type, overloads), then send DONE and release. You then take ownership, pull the current file state, and layer your process_priority param on top. This avoids simultaneous clobbering. OK with you going second, or do you need to go first instead?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: We both need the same 2 files (Editor.__init__ and edit() signature overlap) - true disjoint split isn't possible. Proposing sequential handoff instead: my process_priority change is small and additive (one new optional kwarg threaded through Editor.__init__ and edit()). I'll implement it now, keep the diff minimal/isolated to reduce conflict surface, and send DONE with a summary of exact lines touched. You then layer your edit_file->edit_files change on top, reading the post-my-edit file state so you don't clobber it. If you'd rather go first instead, say so now and I'll wait for your DONE before touching anything. Otherwise I'll proceed immediately.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed - you go first with process_priority change, send DONE with summary of lines touched, then I'll implement multi-file edit support on top of your changes.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed - you go first with multi-file edit support, send DONE when finished, I'll then pull current state and layer process_priority on top. I will not touch these files until I see your DONE.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
your_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: There's a mix-up: your REVISE said YOU (agent1) would go first with multi-file edit support, but your ACCEPT says I (agent2) should go first instead. Let's settle it explicitly: I will go first now with the small process_priority change, since you last confirmed that. I'm starting now and will send DONE with an exact summary of lines/functions touched when finished. Please hold off editing _termui_impl.py and termui.py until you see my DONE message.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished multi-file edit support. Summary of changes: (1) _termui_impl.py: renamed Editor.edit_file(filename: str) -> edit_files(filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]), joins multiple quoted filenames in the subprocess command; Editor.edit() now calls self.edit_files((name,)) instead of self.edit_file(name). (2) termui.py: edit()'s filename param type is now 'str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None'; body normalizes filename into a tuple (single str wrapped, iterable consumed via tuple()) and calls ed.edit_files(filenames) instead of ed.edit_file(filename). Editor.__init__ signature untouched by me. All yours now - feel free to layer process_priority on top of Editor.__init__/get_editor/edit_files and the edit() function signature.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Finished process_priority feature. Summary of exact changes so you can build on top cleanly: (1) _termui_impl.py: added module-level constants _NICE_VALUES and _WIN_PRIORITY_CLASSES plus a new free function _set_windows_process_priority(pid, priority_class), inserted right before 'class Editor:' (around line 481). (2) Editor.__init__ signature gained a new trailing kwarg 'process_priority: str | None = None' and a matching 'self.process_priority = process_priority' assignment - these are the LAST parameter/assignment in the constructor, so appending your own new params after mine (e.g. for edit_files) should be conflict-free if you also append at the end. (3) Added a new method Editor._apply_process_priority(self, pid) right after __init__ and before get_editor(). (4) In edit_file(), added one line 'self._apply_process_priority(c.pid)' immediately after the subprocess.Popen(...) call and before 'exit_code = c.wait()'. I did NOT touch Editor.edit() or edit_file()'s signature/body otherwise, so if your multi-file support changes edit_file's signature or adds edit_files(), those are free to add. (5) termui.py: edit() gained a new trailing param 'process_priority: str | None = None' (appended after 'filename'), a new :param: docstring entry, and the Editor(...) call was reformatted to multi-line kwargs including process_priority=process_priority - just add your new params/kwargs alongside mine there. Diff is now in patch.txt. Releasing the files to you - go ahead when ready.
